summary: Iteration 3.1 re-verification. Both previously-failing items now PASS. Backend: 59/59 pytest tests green (entire suite: backend_test.py + test_iteration3.py). Brute-force lockout with X-Forwarded-For: 9.9.9.9 header triggers 429 by the 9th attempt (verified). Added a new test_lockout_with_ip_rotation which rotates X-Forwarded-For each attempt (10.0.0.1 .. 10.0.0.24) and verifies the username-only secondary counter (threshold 2*BRUTE_FORCE_LIMIT=16) still locks out. Frontend: admin-logout-btn now does logout() then window.location.href='/' — verified the browser lands on '/' (landing page, NOT /admin). Auth guard then correctly redirects /admin/dashboard back to /admin after logout, and localStorage codex_token is cleared. No regressions to other previously-passing tests.
critical_code_review_comments: get_client_ip() correctly prefers X-Forwarded-For (first token), falls back to X-Real-IP, then request.client.host — good standard ordering.

check_brute_force uses two counters: per (ip:username) key with BRUTE_FORCE_LIMIT=8, and per-username-only with threshold 2*BRUTE_FORCE_LIMIT=16. This defeats IP rotation while keeping some tolerance for legitimate shared-IP retries.
clear_login_attempts wipes BOTH the ip:username key document and all username-matching docs via $or — successful login properly resets both counters.
AdminDashboard.doLogout now uses window.location.href='/' for a hard redirect, side-stepping the RequireAuth <Navigate to='/admin'> race condition entirely. Clean fix.
Minor: consider also caching/truncating X-Forwarded-For more defensively if abuse via injected header occurs (unlikely behind trusted ingress), but not a blocker.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration3.py (TestBruteForce: now sends X-Forwarded-For: 9.9.9.9 and asserts lockout by attempt 9; added test_lockout_with_ip_rotation to exercise the username-only secondary counter)
seed_data_creation: TEST_ prefixed members/applications/files/categories are created and cleaned up by tests. login_attempts rows auto-expire via 1h TTL.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iteration 3 items now green. The TestBruteForce class sets X-Forwarded-For explicitly so it is deterministic across environments. Run via: export $(grep -v '^#' /app/frontend/.env | xargs) && pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v
rca of the issue: Previous logout bug — RequireAuth's <Navigate to='/admin'> replaced the client-side navigate('/'); fixed by doing a full-page window.location.href='/' reload after logout() so React Router state is discarded before the guard runs. Previous brute-force bug — slowapi's get_remote_address used the ingress pod IP which rotated across replicas so ip:username counter never reached threshold; fixed by reading X-Forwarded-For first in get_client_ip AND adding a username-only secondary counter at threshold 2*BRUTE_FORCE_LIMIT.
